mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output that improves near-term corporate cash flow expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations during tightening cycles, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to generate a statistically significant negative return differential.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth indicates expanding economic output that directly increases corporate earnings potential, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods produce negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the series shows a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a contraction in manufacturing activity that reduces near-term earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict negative returns with a p-value below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.
